pattern: Rabbits giving the same answer can share a color group of a fixed size, so the minimum total comes from packing each answer value into as few full groups as possible.
hints: Rabbits that report the same number can belong to the same color, but only up to a limited group size.
If a rabbit says k, its color group holds exactly k+1 rabbits, so count how many gave each answer and fill groups of that capacity.
For each distinct answer k with count c, the number of groups needed is ceil(c / (k+1)); each group contributes k+1 rabbits to the total.

edge_inputs.description: two share a small group, one needs its own larger group
more rabbits than one group of that answer can hold
single rabbit answering zero is alone in its color
all answer zero, each is its own color
exactly fills one group with no remainder
one extra rabbit forces a second full group
mixed answers each in separate color groups
maximum answer value, single rabbit implies a huge color group

stress.description: large array of random small answers so many groups partially fill
stress.note: small answer range maximizes group packing remainders

stress.description: answers spread across the full value range so each group is large and sparse
stress.note: wide range stresses large group capacities

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def numRabbits(self, answers):
        from collections import Counter
        cnt = Counter(answers)
        total = 0
        for k, c in cnt.items():
            group = k + 1
            groups = 0
            remaining = c
            while remaining > 0:
                groups += 1
                remaining -= group
            total += groups * group
        return total
